relationshipRules.title: Parent → child rules (draft)
relationshipRules.hint: Validation requires a path from the root type to every other type, and every live tree edge must be an allowed pair. Add each parent type that may contain each child type (e.g. SITE → ZONE).
relationshipRules.empty: No relationship rules in this draft yet.
relationshipRules.duplicatePair: This parent → child rule already exists.
relationshipRules.countHint: {{count}} rules in this draft — expand to add or review.

createNode.hintRoot: A new top-level node is created under the active (published) structure model, using the active type and relationship rules.
createNode.noAllowedTypes: No node type is allowed in this context. For a child, add a relationship rule on the draft (parent type → this child type) and ensure an active model exists with a matching rule when creating live nodes. For a root, you need a root type on the active model.
createNode.missingParent: Select a parent in the tree before adding a child (or add a root node).

lifecycle.draftModeBanner: You are editing a draft. The published organization is unchanged until you run a successful publish (with step-up if required).
lifecycle.publishedViewBanner: Read-only: you are viewing the live, published structure. To change types, rules, or make structural moves, start or switch to a draft (org.admin).
lifecycle.noModelYet: No structure model exists yet. Create the first draft, then add node types and rules before publishing.
lifecycle.publishedNoDraft: A published model is active. To change the org schema, create a new draft that copies the current published node types and relationship rules. You will not edit the live org until you publish the draft.
lifecycle.bootstrapDialogBody: This creates a new empty draft (versioned). You will add node types and relationship rules, then publish when ready. This path is only for the first model — when a published model already exists, use “New draft from published” instead.
lifecycle.forkDialogBody: This creates a new draft and copies all node type definitions and relationship rules from the current published model. The live org tree is unchanged until you publish and pass validation. You can describe this draft in the optional field below.
lifecycle.abandonDialogBody: The draft will be archived. This does not change the published organization. You can create another draft later.
lifecycle.prePublishDraftOnly: Pre-publish: only a draft exists. Add types and rules, then publish to go live.

designer.title: Organization Designer
designer.noActiveModelHint: Create and publish a structure model in the configuration section before designing the organization tree.
designer.searchPlaceholder: Search by code, name, or type…
designer.addChildNodeNeedSelection: Select a parent node in the tree first.
designer.noActiveModelForNodes: Live nodes require a published (active) structure model. When only a draft exists, complete types, rules, and the first publish — then you can add sites and lines here.
designer.manageTypesNoDraftHint: Create a draft and switch to Draft (editing) mode first.
designer.manageTypesReadOnlyHint: Switch to Draft (editing) mode to change node types.
designer.readOnlyInspectorBanner: Read-only: select nodes to browse details. Switch to Draft (editing) to change equipment, run actions, or change types.

metadata.title: Node details (live data)
metadata.name: Name
metadata.versionConflict: The node was changed elsewhere. We refreshed the form — try your edit again.

inspector.noSelection: Select a node in the tree to view its details
inspector.noEquipment: No equipment assigned to this node
inspector.actionsDescription: Preview the impact of structural changes before committing them.

nodeTypes.title: Node Type Manager
nodeTypes.description: Define node types with icons, colors, and capability flags.

governance.readyToPublish: Ready to publish ({{remapCount}} node type remap(s))

preview.title: Impact Preview
preview.acknowledgeWarnings: I acknowledge the warnings and wish to proceed

actionLabel.MoveNode: Move node to a new parent
actionLabel.ReassignResponsibility: Reassign responsibility to another person or team
